pubmed_links: [Baglietto et al. (2014) RORB gene and 9q21.13 microdeletion: report on a patient with epilepsy and mild intellectual disability](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/24355400/); [Rudolf et al. (2016) Loss of function of the retinoid-related nuclear receptor (RORB) gene and epilepsy](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27352968/); [McGrath et al. (2009) Evidence for genetic association of RORB with bipolar disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19909500/)
summary: RORB encodes a nuclear receptor transcription factor highly expressed in the brain; loss-of-function variants and microdeletions cause genetic generalized epilepsy with intellectual disability, and common intronic variants have been associated with bipolar disorder risk. (emerging evidence)
